Title: Must-Have Tools on Ubuntu
Description: Essential utilities and productivity tools every Ubuntu system should have

Sections.Content:
## Must-Have Ubuntu Command Line Tools
This wiki lists essential utilities for any Ubuntu system and how to install them.
### Install all essential tools at once

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y \
  nano \
  vim \
  htop \
  btop \
  curl \
  wget \
  git \
  net-tools \
  nmap \
  ncdu \
  tree \
  tmux \
  screen \
  unzip \
  zip \
  rsync \
  jq \
  build-essential \
  python3-pip \
  lsof \
  iotop \
  dstat \
  mtr \
  traceroute \
  sysstat

## Tool Reference

### Text Editors
- **nano** – Simple, beginner-friendly terminal editor
- **vim** – Powerful, keyboard-driven editor (steep learning curve)

### System Monitoring
- **htop** – Interactive process viewer (better than top)
- **btop** – Modern, colorful resource monitor
- **iotop** – Monitor disk I/O by process
- **dstat** – Combined CPU, memory, disk, network statistics
- **sysstat (sar, iostat)** – System activity reporter

### Network Tools
- **net-tools (ifconfig, netstat)** – Classic network utilities
- **nmap** – Network port scanner
- **mtr** – Combines ping + traceroute
- **traceroute** – Trace network path to a host

### File Management
- **ncdu** – Disk usage analyzer with ncurses UI
- **tree** – Display directory structure as a tree
- **rsync** – Fast file synchronization
- **unzip/zip** – Archive management

### Multiplexers
- **tmux** – Terminal multiplexer (multiple panes, sessions)
- **screen** – Alternative terminal multiplexer

### Development
- **git** – Version control
- **jq** – JSON processor
- **build-essential** – GCC, make, and other build tools
- **python3-pip** – Python package manager
